document.title: AS/NZS Test Report for 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module

sourceSummary.oneLine: AS/NZS 4268:2017 test report AR740701-11AC for Laird Connectivity model ST60-2230C 2.4 GHz WLAN/Bluetooth module family.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory evidence that the tested sample was evaluated against AS/NZS 4268:2017 and EN 300 328 V2.2.2 test requirements, with reported pass results for listed transmitter, receiver, adaptivity, and receiver blocking tests.
keyPoints: Report No. AR740701-11AC, Rev. 01, initial issue Feb. 25, 2021.
Equipment is identified as an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module; model ST60-2230C was selected as the representative model for final test.
The report states the tested sample was evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of AS/NZS 4268:2017.

Summary of Test Results reports Pass for RF Output Power, Power Spectral Density, Adaptivity, Emission Bandwidth, Operating Frequencies, transmitter unwanted emissions, receiver spurious emissions, and Receiver Blocking.

entities.name: International Certification Corp.
entities.evidence: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

entities.name: EN 300 328 V2.2.2 (2019-07)
entities.evidence: According to the specifications of the manufacturer, the EUT must comply with the requirements of the following standards: AS/NZS 4268:2017 EN 300 328 V2.2.2 (2019-07)

concepts.name: Supplementary test report
concepts.evidence: This report is issued as a supplementary report to original ICC report no. AR740701-03AC.

concepts.name: Representative model testing
concepts.evidence: The above models, model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

concepts.name: Power Spectral Density
concepts.evidence: Power Spectral Density Meet the requirement of limit. Pass

concepts.name: Emission Bandwidth and Operating Frequencies
concepts.evidence: Emission Bandwidth Meet the requirement of limit. Pass; Operating Frequencies Meet the requirement of limit. Pass

concepts.name: Adaptivity
concepts.evidence: Adaptivity Meet the requirement of limit. Pass

concepts.name: Receiver Blocking
concepts.evidence: Receiver Blocking Meet the requirement of limit. Pass

concepts.name: Receiver Spurious Emissions
concepts.evidence: Receiver spurious emissions Meet the requirement of limit. Pass

evidence.claim: International Certification Corp. declared the tested sample evaluated and compliant with the above standards.
evidence.evidenceText: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

evidence.claim: The report is a supplementary report to ICC report no. AR740701-03AC.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is issued as a supplementary report to original ICC report no. AR740701-03AC.

evidence.claim: The modifications include additional models/product names, resistance differences for CONFIG_HOST0/1/2, latest software version, new applicant and brand name, and one added antenna.
evidence.evidenceText: The modification is 1. Additional model and product names 2. Resistance of CONFIG_HOST0/1/2 are different to switch interface 3. Update to the latest SW version 4. New applicant name & brand name. 5. Adding one antenna.

evidence.claim: The report states the modifications did not impact original test results.
evidence.evidenceText: After verification, above changes does not impact to original test results. Thus test results remain the same.

evidence.claim: ST60-2230C was selected as the representative model for final testing.
evidence.evidenceText: The above models, model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

evidence.claim: The product model family listed in the report includes ST60-2230C, ST60-2230C-P, ST60-2230C-PU, ST60-2230C-SS, ST60-2230C-U, ST60-2230C-UU, and ST60-2230C-Q.

evidence.claim: The EUT supports 2400-2483.5 MHz 802.11 b/g/n HT20 operation and 802.11n HT40 operation from 2422-2462 MHz.
RF Output Power summary result was 19.86 dBm and Pass.

reviewFlags.reason: Authority is not stated as an issuing approval authority; the source references AS/NZS, ACMA 4268 clauses, and standards rather than a certificate or regulator decision.
Several radiated emissions result pages contain headings and notes but no extracted measured result rows, suggesting incomplete OCR/text extraction for plotted or tabular data.
Document date is taken from the Release Record issued date; the cover page also contains received and tested dates.
